reasoning_model_1: The model demonstrates excellent performance. 1. Content Accuracy: The spoken content perfectly matches the script '比赛只剩下最后几分钟了，我们必须得分！快传球，射门……糟糕，球被抢走了！', keeping all intended punctuation and phrasing. 2. Fluency and Naturalness: The speech is fluent. 3. Paralinguistic Compliance: The model successfully executes the two-part instruction. It starts with a rapid, urgent pace for '比赛只剩下最后几分钟了，我们必须得分！', creating tension. Then, it transitions effectively to the required 'intermittent' (断续) style for '快传球，射门……糟糕……', utilizing the pauses represented by the ellipses to convey the halting realization of the loss. The transition is distinct and adheres strictly to the prompt.
reasoning_model_2: The model performs poorly. 1. Content Accuracy: There are noticeable issues. The phrase '必须得分' is broken strangely into '必须得 分', and the subsequent text '快传球' is run together with the previous sentence without appropriate separation. Furthermore, the critical pauses indicated by '……' in the prompt are missing, resulting in '射门糟糕' running together. 2. Paralinguistic Compliance: The model fails to implement the 'intermittent' (断续) instruction in the second half. By removing the necessary pauses/ellipses, the rhythm remains flat or confusing, failing to contrast with the opening pace. The sudden shift in rhythm is not effectively conveyed.
model_comparison: Model 1 is the clear winner. It accurately synthesizes the text with the correct punctuation and, crucially, follows the complex paralinguistic instruction to shift from a rapid pace to an intermittent/halting style. The transition is marked and appropriate to the context of the sport commentary. Model 2 fails to adhere to the prosodic requirements; it misses the specific pauses (ellipses) required for the 'intermittent' section, resulting in run-on sentences ('射门糟糕') and unnatural breaks ('得 分'). Model 1 demonstrates superior control over dynamic adjustments in rhythm.
